name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans, retail sales, and gold buying services.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn shop chain with over 3,300 retail locations. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need quick access to funds or affordable merchandise through collateral-based lending. FirstCash operates across 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador), employing approximately 22,000 people globally. The company is publicly traded and included in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index.

FirstCash's core business model centers on non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property—customers deposit items of value to receive immediate cash loans. The company also buys and sells a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Additional services include gold and precious metal buying, layaway plans with 10% down payments, and retail sales of inventory. Through its subsidiary AFF, FirstCash also operates lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across 15,000+ merchant partner locations.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale and international presence, operating significantly more locations than most competitors. The company emphasizes the non-recourse nature of pawn loans, meaning customers' personal liability is limited to the pledged item itself. Their inventory model—buying and selling merchandise in-store—creates a unique retail component that extends beyond traditional lending. The company positions itself as serving underbanked consumers without requiring credit checks or employment verification.

However, customers should understand that pawn loans involve surrendering physical possessions as collateral, and failure to repay results in loss of the item. While non-recourse, the effective cost of pawn loans can be high when calculated as an annual percentage rate, particularly for short-term borrowing. The company's broad retail sales model means inventory quality and item availability varies significantly by location. Additionally, pawn loans do not build credit history, making them unsuitable for consumers seeking to improve their credit profile.
pros: Non-recourse pawn loans mean customers' liability is limited to the pledged item, with no deficiency judgments
Immediate cash funding on the spot with no credit checks, background checks, or employment verification required
3,300+ retail locations across 29 U.S. states, DC, UK, and Latin America provide extensive accessibility
No monthly payments required—customers can repay on their timeline to retrieve items
Gold and precious metals buying service provides instant cash for jewelry and bullion
Layaway service with only 10% down payment makes retail purchases more affordable
Wide variety of merchandise available for purchase in-store at negotiated prices
cons: Pledged items are lost permanently if the loan is not repaid, making this unsuitable for items with sentimental value
Pawn loans do not report to credit bureaus and do not build credit history or help improve credit scores
Interest rates and fees on pawn loans, while typically lower than payday loans, can equate to very high APRs for short-term borrowing periods
Inventory varies significantly by location, and availability of specific items is not guaranteed
The retail aspect means customers may overpay for merchandise compared to market value
best_for: Consumers with valuable items who need immediate cash but want to avoid high-APR payday or title loans
Credit-constrained or unbanked individuals who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit products
People seeking to buy used merchandise at negotiated prices or hunting for specific items
Those with gold, jewelry, or precious metals they want to convert to cash quickly
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for individuals who own valuable collateral and need immediate cash without credit checks or lengthy approval processes, particularly those unable to qualify for traditional bank loans. The primary caveat is that pawn loans require surrendering physical possessions as collateral—failure to repay means permanent loss of the item—and these loans do not build credit history, making them unsuitable as a credit-building tool.
services: Non-recourse pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, etc.)
Gold, silver, and platinum buying—customers receive cash on the spot
Retail sales of used and new merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, and appliances
Layaway plans with 10% down payment option for retail purchases
Store locator and inventory search services
Lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions (through AFF subsidiary)
Pawn FAQs and educational resources about how pawn loans work
Buy, sell, and trade services for merchandise
